metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace or augment the vague 'reduced' success criterion with a measurable proxy (e.g., 'a carryover finding on UNCHANGED code is dropped and labeled rather than re-surfaced, verifiable via the AC3 test harness').
findings.finding: The first Success Criterion ('Repeat-noise across code-review runs is reduced') is not measurable — no baseline, threshold, or proxy metric is stated. An executor completing the work cannot determine whether this criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Success Criteria, bullet 1
findings.suggested_fix: Restate as: 'On a re-run with no code changes, all prior low-priority surfaced findings are labeled as carried-over and not re-coached — verifiable by the AC3 region_gated_floor test.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the novelty sub-call for code review reuses the existing `plan-novelty` reviewer ID (with {{plan}} removed or replaced by a diff/context block) or registers a new `code-review-novelty` reviewer entry; either way, add the reviewer definition to `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/`.
findings.finding: The plan states code review 'calls both UNCHANGED' (the novelty sub-call from verify.py and rising_floor_drop from decide.py), but the existing novelty reviewer prompt `plan_review_novelty.md` contains the plan-review-specific template variable `{{plan}}` and is titled 'Plan-review Pass-2 novelty sub-call'. No code-review novelty reviewer prompt or reviewer ID exists. The plan does not specify whether a new reviewer entry is needed or how the existing prompt is adapted.
findings.location: Design section — 'REUSE, do not duplicate. The shared review kernel already holds the two pure primitives: the novelty sub-call contract (review_kernel/verify.py) and rising_floor_drop(priority, novelty) (review_kernel/decide.py). Code review calls both UNCHANGED.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign ownership of the plan-review gate's surfaced-only fix (old-frilly-plankton) to an existing or new child so that AC5's 'both gates agree' outcome is fully covered.
findings.finding: AC5 ('fixed so both gates agree') implies that the plan-review gate's novelty prior set is also fixed to surfaced-only (old-frilly-plankton affected both gates), but no child in the sibling roster owns the plan-review side of this fix. c639 fixes only the code-review reader; the plan-review gate's definition is unaddressed, leaving the 'both gates agree' outcome uncovered.
findings.location: child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 (AC5 coverage gap; plan-review side unowned by any sibling)
findings.suggested_fix: Either extend c639's scope to also patch the plan-review gate's `latest_review_result` / novelty prior assembly to filter to surfaced-only, or add a fifth child explicitly scoped to 'Fix plan-review novelty prior set to surfaced-only (old-frilly-plankton)' and reference it from AC5.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the region-gate tri-state as named constants (e.g. `REGION_UNCHANGED`, `REGION_CHANGED`, `REGION_UNKNOWN`) in a code-review-specific module (or review_kernel/decide.py), co-located with the `region_gated_floor` decision rule that consumes them.
findings.finding: The tri-state `UNCHANGED / CHANGED / UNKNOWN` that gates region_gated_floor drops is referenced throughout the Design section but its value vocabulary is never formally defined as an enum or constant — no schema file, no decide.py constant, no structured-output field anchors it. Unlike NOVELTY_SUBANSWERS (defined in decide.py, mirrored in novelty_model() and the reviewer prompt), the tri-state exists only in plan prose.
findings.location: Design section — 'Tri-state UNCHANGED / CHANGED / UNKNOWN; only UNCHANGED enables a drop; UNKNOWN behaves as raise'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to review_kernel/decide.py (or code_review/region_gate.py): `REGION_UNCHANGED = "unchanged"`, `REGION_CHANGED = "changed"`, `REGION_UNKNOWN = "unknown"`, and reference these constants in the region_gated_floor predicate and in the hash-map comparison function.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the local artifact title format (e.g. `f'code-review: session:{session_id}'`) and confirm whether session_id appears in the artifact title (enabling O(1) lookup by title match) or only in the sidecar payload (requiring a scan), so the reader's data-flow is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The data-flow from write to read for session-keyed local artifacts is underspecified: AC2 requires `session_id` be added to the `code_review_result_v1` payload (currently absent from `build_payload` in `sidecar.py`) and AC1 requires the reader to query by session_id, but the plan gives no artifact title scheme or payload index field that enables efficient lookup. The existing voter-path lookup works by title `f'code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}'` — that pattern does not extend to local session-keyed artifacts (which have no change_id). Without a specified title format or sidecar index, the reader for local artifacts must full-scan all `code_review` tickets and filter by `session_id` field in the sidecar event, but sidecar events are stored as ticket event files, not indexed fields — making the lookup both unspecified and potentially slow.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 ('A reader loads prior SURFACED code-review findings by typed key (session/change) plus the reviewed-file content-hash map') and AC2 ('the code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id and {path: sha256}')
findings.suggested_fix: Use `f'code-review: session:{session_id}'` as the local artifact title (analogous to the Gerrit `f'code-review: {change_id} @ {revision}'` pattern), and store session_id as a top-level field in `build_payload`; the reader then queries `list_tickets(ticket_type='code_review')` and matches on title prefix `'code-review: session:{session_id}'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the intended behavior when `resolve_session_id()` returns None: if the uuid-per-invocation fallback is intentional (i.e. v1 local memory only works within a harness session), document this scope limitation explicitly; if accumulation across standalone invocations is desired, specify a stable local key (e.g. repo-root hash + branch name) as the fallback instead of a per-invocation uuid.
findings.finding: The uuid-per-invocation fallback for local session keying makes the local novelty path silently inert for standalone CLI usage: when `resolve_session_id()` returns None (no harness env var — the common case for developers running `rebar review-code` outside a coding-agent session), each invocation gets a distinct uuid key, so the reader never finds a prior run to load and `region_gated_floor` never fires. This defeats the stated goal of 'reducing repeat-noise across local iterations.'
findings.location: Design section — 'Local reviews are keyed on the session id (resolve_session_id(), with a per-invocation uuid fallback when it returns None)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact field name and value schema for the carryover label (e.g. `"carried_from": {"run": <session_id|change_id>, "patchset": N}`) and confirm it is included in both the surfaced finding dict and the sidecar persistence slice.
findings.finding: The carryover label 'carried run/patchset N' (AC3) is never given a concrete field name in the finding dict schema. The plan says carryover findings are 'labeled' and 're-coaching is skipped', but no field key (e.g. `_carried`, `carryover_label`, `carried_from`) is specified, and the plan_review sidecar's `_slim()` function has no such field either — so there is no existing analog to reuse.
findings.location: AC3 — 'labels carryover findings (carried run/patchset N) and skips re-coaching'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fallback behavior for region_gated_floor when the prior-artifact reader or file-hasher raises (e.g. 'any reader/hasher exception resolves the affected region to UNKNOWN, which behaves as raise — no drop'), mirroring the plan_review fail-safe pattern.
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `region_gated_floor` wraps `rising_floor_drop` but gives no fallback for failure of the reader step (loading the prior artifact, hashing current files, or building the hash-map). The plan_review analog (`_score_floor_novelty` and `_maybe_apply_rising_floor`) both have explicit `try/except → no-drop` fail-safes (lines 175-176, 201-202 of plan_review/__init__.py). The plan is silent on what `region_gated_floor` does if the artifact read throws, the hash-map is malformed, or the sha256 computation fails.
findings.location: Design section — 'A new region_gated_floor (code_review-specific) wraps rising_floor_drop with a third conjunct.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Design section: 'Any exception in the reader or hash step resolves all affected regions to UNKNOWN (raise); region_gated_floor is wrapped in a try/except that returns the full unfiltered finding set on any error.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 3bff as an explicit dependency of e51b, or note in e51b's scope that local-path end-to-end tests require 3bff to be merged first.
findings.finding: e51b's 'Wire into BOTH gate paths (local + Gerrit voter)' implicitly depends on 3bff having delivered local persistence, but e51b names only c639 as a dependency ('Depends on the reader/payload story'). Without 3bff completed, the local path has no prior artifact to supply to the reader, making the local convergence path of e51b a no-op (first-run-forever behavior) and leaving the e51b AC 'Wire into BOTH gate paths' effectively untestable for the local path end-to-end.
findings.location: child e51b-bf7c-63e0-4d06 (dependency gap on 3bff)
findings.suggested_fix: Add '3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33' to e51b's Depends section alongside c639. Alternatively, add a note in e51b's AC: 'Local-path integration tests require 3bff; unit tests for region_gated_floor logic can proceed independently with a mocked reader.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the storage backend for local `rebar review-code` artifacts in AC2 or the Design section (e.g., 'same git-shared artifact store as Gerrit, keyed on session:<uuid>' or 'local filesystem at <path>').
findings.finding: The persistence mechanism for local code_review artifacts is unspecified. The plan states that local `rebar review-code` must persist a code_review artifact (AC2) and that these are keyed on session_id, but does not say WHERE or HOW the artifact is stored locally — same git-shared store as Gerrit artifacts? A local filesystem path? A different backend entirely? An executor cannot implement this without choosing a storage mechanism that the plan leaves open.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC2 / Design § Keying
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Design §Keying: 'Local artifacts are written to the same git-shared artifact store as Gerrit artifacts, using key session:<session_id>; the git-shared store is therefore the single persistence backend for both keyspaces.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies ADR 0008 Invariant 1 is preserved: e.g. 'The Pass-1 finder call-site receives no prior findings; prior findings are accessible only at the post-find novelty seam.'
findings.finding: The Design section explicitly preserves ADR 0008 Invariant 1 ('the Pass-1 finder never sees prior findings; only the post-find novelty seam does') as a required maintained constraint, but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that this invariant is not violated by the new reader/gating wiring.
findings.location: Design section ('Neutrality preserved') — no corresponding AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'The Pass-1 code-review finder is not passed prior findings at any call-site; the region_gated_floor / novelty seam is the only code path that consumes the loaded prior findings.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an expand-contract sequence or reader-side fallback (treat missing `session_id`/hash-map as UNKNOWN/no-prior) and a rollback path for the `code_review_result_v1` schema extension.
findings.finding: The plan extends the `code_review_result_v1` payload with new fields (`session_id`, `{path: sha256}`) without acknowledging the schema migration for existing git-shared artifacts already written without those fields, and provides no expand-contract sequence or rollback path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id and {path: sha256}'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC and Design sections, explicitly state: (1) the reader treats a missing `session_id` or missing path-hash map as equivalent to 'no prior run' (UNKNOWN for all regions); (2) old artifacts remain valid and are not rewritten; (3) the rollback path is simply deploying old code, which will ignore the new fields it never writes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either update ADR 0008 to cover code-review novelty (keyspace isolation, region_gated_floor contract, tri-state semantics) or create a new ADR for the code-review novelty gate, and reference it in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural contract — `region_gated_floor` with tri-state region semantics, typed keyspaces (session:<id> vs change:<id>), and the extension of ADR 0008 Invariant 1 to code review — without calling out a new ADR or an update to ADR 0008.
findings.location: Design section — 'region_gated_floor', 'TYPED keyspaces', 'Neutrality preserved: … ADR 0008 Invariant 1'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Capture the region-gated novelty floor pattern, typed keyspace design, and code_review_result_v1 schema contract in a new ADR (or an amendment to ADR 0008) so future review surfaces and schema evolutions have a discoverable, authoritative reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural pattern (region-gated novelty floor, typed keyspaces, content-hash region tracking, code_review_result_v1 schema carrying session_id and {path:sha256}) that extends ADR 0008 to code review with a new gate and a new cross-component artifact contract, but names no ADR or design-doc update.
findings.location: Design (settled) section; Acceptance Criteria (AC lines 1–2)
findings.suggested_fix: Create ADR-XXXX 'Code-review novelty gate and artifact schema' referencing ADR 0008, documenting: (1) the region-gate tri-state and content-hash approach, (2) the typed keyspace contract (session:/change: prefixes and isolation rule), (3) the code_review_result_v1 schema with its required fields, and (4) the v1→v2 evolution path (file-level → line-level). Link it from CLAUDE.md.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a measurement step (e.g. a pilot run logging drop-rate by region-state and priority band, or a RED fixture asserting expected drop counts on a canned multi-patchset scenario) to validate that the region gate + rising_floor_drop combination achieves the stated noise-reduction goal before full rollout.
findings.finding: The plan's core value claim — 'repeat-noise is reduced' — is asserted with no measurement plan: no before/after finding-count baseline, no success threshold, and no pilot metric to confirm the region gate + rising_floor_drop combination achieves meaningful convergence in practice for code review (as opposed to plan review, where ADR 0008 was validated).
findings.location: Success Criteria section
findings.suggested_fix: Instrument region_gated_floor to log (at debug level) per-run counts of: findings dropped (UNCHANGED+novel+low-priority), findings raised (CHANGED or UNKNOWN), and carryover labels applied. Add a fixture that replays a two-patchset scenario with known file hashes and asserts the expected drop/raise/label distribution. Review counts after the first real Gerrit pilot run before declaring success.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate downstream callers of code_review_result_v1 (Gerrit voter, CI pipeline, report renderers) and confirm the new `label`/drop behavior is backward-compatible or update those callers explicitly.
findings.finding: The plan adds output labeling ("carried run/patchset N") to code-review findings and changes drop behavior, but does not acknowledge downstream callers that currently consume code_review_result_v1 payloads and depend on the existing output schema/format. If the Gerrit voter, CI pipeline, or any report renderer parses the finding list, the new label field and drop behavior are breaking changes that need caller acknowledgment.
findings.location: AC3 / Design section 'Neutrality preserved'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Caller impact' subsection to the Design noting which consumers of code_review_result_v1 are affected by (a) the new carried-finding label field and (b) findings now being silently dropped; confirm schema is additive-only or version the payload.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation mechanism — e.g., 'running review-code twice on the same unchanged codebase, the second run surfaces zero findings that were already surfaced in the first run at the same or lower priority' — to make the noise-reduction claim falsifiable.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism is specified for the primary success criterion ('repeat-noise is reduced'). All ACs are purely structural/mechanical; none define an observable before/after comparison, a suppressed-finding count threshold, or a dogfooding scenario that would confirm the feature actually reduces noise in practice.
findings.location: Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Integration test: given a prior artifact with surfaced findings F on files with content-hashes H, a second run on the same files produces no output for novel=false low-priority findings on UNCHANGED regions, confirmed by asserting the returned finding list excludes those items.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the `_hash_file` reuse mechanism: either extract it to `review_kernel` (the shared kernel both gates already use) so code_review can import it without depending on `plan_review` internals, or explicitly state 'mirror the implementation' in the design — and update AC2 to name the concrete call site.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file' for the per-file content-hash map, but _hash_file is a private function inside src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py — a plan-review-specific module. The plan does not specify whether 'reuse' means import across the module boundary (creating an unintended coupling from code_review to plan_review internals) or copy the logic into a code-review module/shared location. This leaves the reuse mechanism unresolved.
findings.location: Design section — 'store a per-reviewed-file content-hash map {path: sha256} in the artifact (reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file)'
findings.suggested_fix: Move `_hash_file` (or an equivalent `hash_file(path, *, base)`) into `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/` (e.g. `review_kernel/hashes.py`) so both plan-review and code-review can import it from the shared kernel without crossing module privacy boundaries. Alternatively, if the function stays in `plan_review/attest.py`, change its name to `hash_file` (public) and document the intended cross-module use.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the reader's forward-compatibility contract for old-format artifacts lacking `session_id` or the hash map (e.g., treat absent hash map as all-UNKNOWN, treat absent session_id as unkeyed/skip-novelty), and document the post-rollback schema state so the artifact store is in a known-valid state regardless of deployment outcome.
findings.finding: The plan extends the `code_review_result_v1` payload with new fields (`session_id`, `{path: sha256}`) but does not specify how the reader handles pre-existing artifacts that lack these fields, and does not describe an expand-contract or graceful-degradation path for the schema transition.
findings.location: AC line: 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id and {path: sha256}' / Design section: 'A reader loads prior SURFACED code-review findings by typed key … plus the reviewed-file content-hash map'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit reader fallback: if `session_id` is absent, treat the artifact as unkeyed (skip novelty path); if `{path: sha256}` is absent or the key is missing for a file, resolve to UNKNOWN (raise). Document that rollback leaves new-format artifacts in the store harmlessly because the old reader applies the same absent-field → UNKNOWN/skip logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether adding session_id and {path: sha256} to code_review_result_v1 is additive (no schema bump) and document how the reader handles artifacts lacking these fields (treat missing path-hash map as UNKNOWN for all files, i.e. same raise behavior as a fresh review).
findings.finding: The plan states 'The code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id and {path: sha256}' (AC2) and references reusing 'attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file', but the current sidecar.build_payload (src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py:64-91) does NOT carry these fields, and SCHEMA is already pinned to 'code_review_result_v1'. The plan does not specify whether the payload extension is additive-only (backward-compatible) or requires a schema version bump, nor does it address how the new reader guards against artifacts written before this change (which will lack session_id and path-hashes), leaving the reader's schema-tolerance behavior unverified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria §2 ('code_review_result_v1 payload carries session_id and {path: sha256}') / Design §Region gate / src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py:21,64-91
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the Design section: 'build_payload extension is additive; a missing or empty path-hash map in a prior artifact resolves every file as UNKNOWN (raise), matching first-review behavior. No schema version bump required.' Add a corresponding reader note to AC1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether _hash_file will be promoted to a shared utility (e.g. review_kernel or a new rebar.llm.hashing module) or imported directly from plan_review.attest, and update the Design section accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts reusing 'plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file' for the per-file content-hash map, but _hash_file is a private symbol (single-underscore prefix) inside plan_review/attest.py. The plan does not specify whether it will be promoted to a shared utility module or imported directly as a private symbol from attest.py, leaving an integration surface ambiguity: a future refactor of attest.py could silently break the code-review reader.
findings.location: Design §Region gate ('reuse plan-review attest.dependency_hashes / _hash_file') / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:88

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit dependency from 3bff on c639 (or sequence them) and document the integration protocol for the shared `produce_code_review_verdict` edit so the deps map is in place before local emit wiring lands.
findings.finding: Both 3bff and c639 independently modify `produce_code_review_verdict` in gate_dispatch.py — 3bff to wire local emit under a typed session key, c639 to compute the deps map centrally — but neither child names the other as a dependency or describes an integration protocol for concurrent edits to the same function body.
findings.location: child 3bff-cdf6-8fca-4c33 and child c639-4122-58d9-4dc2 (shared gate_dispatch.py / produce_code_review_verdict)
findings.suggested_fix: Mark 3bff as depending on c639 in its Scope/Depends section. Alternatively, consolidate the gate_dispatch.py changes into c639 (since it already owns 'compute deps centrally in the gate') and have 3bff only touch _llm_commands.py and shim.py, delegating the emit call to the gate that c639 has already extended.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
